top_stories.title: Microsoft makes passkeys default and retires SMS MFA by 2027
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is making passkeys the default authentication method in Microsoft Entra ID starting September 1, and will retire Microsoft-provided SMS and voice MFA in February 2027. Organizations relying on telephony-based MFA will need to migrate to alternative methods before the February 2027 deadline.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Makes Passkeys Default: What Identity And Security Leaders Need To Do
top_stories.summary: Microsoft announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for all users in Microsoft Entra ID starting September 1, 2026. The change will automatically enable passkey authentication, marking a significant shift in phishing-resistant MFA adoption across the Microsoft identity platform.

top_stories.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
top_stories.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in authentication defenses.

top_stories.title: AI agents are still logging in as humans
top_stories.summary: A report based on anonymized sign-on data from more than 20,000 organizations on the Okta platform examines how AI agents are authenticating using human identity credentials across enterprise environments. The findings highlight identity risks arising from mixed AI tool stacks, where both sanctioned and unsanctioned AI platforms coexist within organizations.

top_stories.title: Oak raised $60M to give every user, machine, and AI agent one identity system
top_stories.summary: Oak, an Israeli identity startup, has emerged from stealth with a $60M seed round to build a unified identity system covering human users, machines, and AI agents. The company is targeting the growing challenge of managing non-human identities alongside traditional user identities within a single platform.

news.title: Week in review: High severity WordPress vulnerabilities, fake OAuth IDs bypass sign-in logs
news.summary: The week-in-review article covers multiple security topics including fake OAuth IDs being used to bypass sign-in logs. OAuth identity spoofing to circumvent authentication logging is directly relevant to IAM professionals monitoring sign-in activity and audit trails.

news.title: Microsoft warns of surge in ACR Stealer attacks on customers
news.summary: Microsoft has observed a surge in ACR Stealer malware attacks targeting enterprise customers to steal browser-stored passwords, authentication tokens, and sensitive documents. The malware specifically targets authentication credentials and tokens, directly impacting identity security for Microsoft's enterprise customer base.

news.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
news.summary: Okta's Red Team discovered and reported a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, dubbed "HollowByte," which allows an 11-byte TLS request to cause an unpatched server to allocate up to 131 KB of memory that is never freed until process restart. OpenSSL shipped a fix in June without a CVE, advisory, or changelog entry referencing the issue, with Okta's Red Team subsequently publishing details of the flaw they identified and named.

news.title: OpenSSL Fixes HollowByte Memory Exhaustion Bug
news.summary: Okta's Red Team discovered and disclosed a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, dubbed HollowByte, involving an 11-byte flaw that allows remote attackers to exhaust server memory. OpenSSL has released a fix for the vulnerability following Okta's responsible disclosure.

news.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
news.summary: A Go-based botnet named NadMesh has been discovered actively scanning for exposed AI services to harvest cloud credentials, with the operator's dashboard claiming 3,811 unique AWS keys collected. The botnet targets services like ComfyUI, Ollama, and Langflow using Shodan to find exposed instances, then extracts cloud keys and Kubernetes tokens from misconfigured deployments.

news.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
news.summary: ACR Stealer, an infostealer active since 2024, is stealing browser passwords, session tokens, and Microsoft 365 documents including files from OneDrive and SharePoint. The malware uses ClickFix social engineering tactics, tricking users into manually executing commands, with Microsoft's Defender Experts team documenting the delivery chains.

news.title: Kuehn Law Encourages Investors of SailPoint, Inc. to Contact Law Firm
news.summary: Kuehn Law, PLLC is investigating whether SailPoint, Inc. officers and directors breached fiduciary duties to shareholders. The investigation is a shareholder litigation inquiry into the IAM/IGA vendor, which trades on NASDAQ under the ticker SAIL.

news.title: AI Security: How AI assistants securely assess your personal data
news.summary: The article explains how AI assistants use OAuth 2.0 On-Behalf-Of (OBO) token delegation to securely access personal data. The piece covers the authorization flow that allows AI systems to act on a user's behalf while maintaining secure access controls.

news.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider hacking group were sentenced to 5.5 years each for the 2024 hack of Transport for London, which compromised 148 systems and forced all 27,000 employees to reset passwords in person. The attack resulted in £29 million in losses and recovery costs for TfL.

news.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
news.summary: A vulnerability in n8n's workflow automation platform allowed attackers to log in as other users by exploiting improper JWT validation on Enterprise instances configured with multiple token issuers. The flaw matched incoming JWTs on the `sub` claim alone while ignoring the `iss` (issuer) claim, meaning a valid token from one issuer could authenticate as a different user registered under another issuer.

news.title: Building Secure APIs with OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect
news.summary: An educational article covers API security implementation using OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect, addressing authentication and authorization flows, token management, PKCE, and security best practices. The content targets developers and IAM professionals working with modern identity protocols and API access management.

news.title: ESDS Launches Swaraj Nandi and Swaraj Hansa, Two Sovereign Enterprise Security Platforms Built Entirely in India - The Tribune
news.summary: ESDS has launched two enterprise security platforms, Swaraj Nandi and Swaraj Hansa, targeting privileged access management and security governance. The platforms are built in India and address privileged access monitoring and security alert management for enterprise environments.

news.title: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
news.summary: Microsoft published a blog post on applying least privilege principles to AI agents, covering identity, access controls, and tool binding. The article addresses how autonomous AI agents require strong identity verification, scoped permissions, and auditing controls to remain secure.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem after a contractor accidentally exposed dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights failures in secrets management, credential monitoring, and contractor access governance relevant to IAM and PAM practices.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and compromise identity systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize high-profile accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions circulating on Telegram showed how to manipulate the AI bot into bypassing normal account authentication and recovery controls.
